content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 30, 1986

Michael W. McBeth, President
Woodside Neighborhood Association
2233 Ridgecrest Way
Pittsburg, CA 94565

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-86-233

Dear Mr. McBeth:

Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of the
Woodside Neighborhood Association regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

The Commission is unable to render formal written advice to
you regarding Supervisor Schroeder's obligations under the
Act. Section 83114 authorizes the Commission to provide
written advice to any person concerning his or her duties under
the Act. Formal written advice may be requested only by the
person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that
person's authorized representative (See, 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18329(b)(8)(B), copy enclosed.)

I have enclosed a Guide to the Political Reform Act which
may assist you in any future inquiries regarding the conflict
of interest law for public officials. If you believe that a
public official has already violated a provision of the Act,
you may wish to file a complaint with the Commission's
Enforcement Division (forms enclosed) or the local District
Attorney's office.

If you have any questions regarding this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:DL:plh
Enclosures

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

FPPC

JUL 17 2 15 PM '86

July 16, 1986


State of California
Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 'K' Street
Sacramento, CA 95804

+++CERTIFIED MAIL+++


RE: CONTRA COSTA COUNTY BOARD OF SUPERVISOR
    ROBERT SCHROEDER
    POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST - LANDFILL VOTE
___________________________________________________________


Gentlemen,

With reference to the above subject matter, we are writing to the
Commission for clarification of the following situation.

Contra Costa County is currently facing a landfill (garbage)
crisis.  All of our landfills are due to reach capacity by the
year 1989.

As of this writing, there are only 3 (three) privately proposed
landfill sites.  They are:

     Proposed Landfill Sites              Private Developer's
     ===========================          ==================

     Antioch/Mira Vista Hills             Sil Garaventa
     Pittsburg/Kirker Pass Road           Sid Corey
     Pittsburg/Bailey Road                Rud Diriey

Of our five County Board of Supervisors, Supervisor Robert
Schroeder, representing Walnut Creek, Lafayette, Danville, San
Ramon, Blackhawk and Pleasanton, we believe has a conflict of
interest in voting on the three proposed landfill sites.

Supervisor Schroeder owns Schroeder Insurance Agency, 1412 N.
Main Street, Walnut Creek, CA 94596, Telephone (415) 934-6789.
His insurance agency insures Valley Disposal's garbage trucks
(solid waste haulers). Valley Disposal will haul garbage to one,
if not all, of the three landfill sites mentioned above.

.../1

State of California
Fair Political Practice Commission
Sacramento, CA
July 15, 1986
Page two

We admit that we have only given the Commission a brief synopsis
of the background history involving a potential conflict.
Nevertheless, we have made reference to all of the above to ask
the Commission the following.

Since Supervisor Schroeder's agency insures a company that will
ultimately utilize one or more of these landfill sites, can
Supervisor Schroeder vote on this issue or must he abstain from
voting due to a possible conflict of interest?

We regret that time is of the essence in this clarification. The
County Board of Supervisor's could vote on the landfills as early
as October of this year.

We would be most grateful to the Commission for investigating and
clarifying this "gray" area of concern.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]

Michael W. McBeth
President, Woodside Neighborhood Association
2233 Ridgecrest Way
Pittsburg, CA 94565
(415) 427-5070

MWM/

cc: Mr. Dennis Cuff - Contra Costa Times
cc: Mr. Tim Roberts - Pittsburg Post Dispatch
cc: Ms. Linda Matsuda - Woodland Hills Neighborhood Association

sections.question_synthetic: The Woodside Neighborhood Association requested formal written advice from the FPPC regarding Supervisor Schroeder's obligations under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice to the Woodside Neighborhood Association because only the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative may request such advice under Section 83114 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC directed the requestor to the Political Reform Act guide and informed them of options to file a complaint if a violation is suspected.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The question and conclusion were synthesized from the body text. Document is a response from FPPC Counsel declining jurisdiction to advise third parties about another person's duties under the Political Reform Act.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The Woodside Neighborhood Association requested formal written advice from the FPPC regarding Supervisor Schroeder's obligations under the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide formal written advice to the Woodside Neighborhood Association because only the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative may request such advice under Section 83114 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC directed the requestor to the Political Reform Act guide and informed them of options to file a complaint if a violation is suspected.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to render formal written advice to the Woodside Neighborhood Association regarding Supervisor Schroeder's conflict of interest obligations, explaining that only the official in question or their authorized representative may request such advice.
